name: Nicotinamide mononucleotide raised NAD metabolites but did not test DNA repair
summary: Human NMN data provide context for NAD-linked DNA-repair hypotheses, but the cited trial does not directly measure DNA repair.
statement: In healthy older men, nicotinamide mononucleotide increased whole-blood NAD+ and related metabolites, but the study did not measure DNA repair endpoints.
quantitative_note: Forty-two participants were randomized; a supplement-supply error led the authors to exclude 12-week data for 22 participants from some analyses.
caveats: This finding is contextual for NAD-linked DNA repair support, not direct evidence of DNA repair enhancement.
The reported motor-function changes were exploratory and require validation.
Some authors were employees of the supplement supplier.
